treasury_yield_pressure.reason: Higher yield pressure can pressure equity multiples through discount-rate and financing channels.

real_yield_pressure.reason: Higher real yields generally tighten financial conditions and pressure long-duration risk assets.

inflation_pressure.reason: Hot inflation can raise rate-path pressure and reduce valuation support.

fed_hawkish_pressure.reason: A hawkish policy path tends to pressure risk assets through tighter financial conditions.

liquidity_pressure.reason: Improving liquidity/financial conditions can support broad risk appetite.

credit_stress.reason: Rising credit stress is a direct risk-off input for equities.

growth_strength.reason: Better growth can support earnings unless it worsens rate pressure.

labor_tightness.reason: Very tight labor data can increase Fed-pressure even when growth appears resilient.

cot_equity_positioning.reason: Positioning can confirm or conflict with risk-asset participation.

eia_energy_balance.reason: Energy inventory rows are not direct equity-index evidence in this pass.

usda_crop_stress.reason: Crop/weather rows are not direct equity-index evidence in this pass.

noaa_weather_hazard.reason: Weather hazards are usually display-only for broad equity indices unless specifically tied to economic disruption.

inflation_pressure.reason: Hot inflation supports upward rate/yield pressure.

fed_hawkish_pressure.reason: A hawkish Fed path supports front-end and broader yield pressure.

growth_strength.reason: Stronger growth can support higher yields through demand and policy expectations.

labor_tightness.reason: Tight labor data can keep rate-pressure elevated.

credit_stress.reason: Risk-off stress may create safe-haven demand for Treasuries, pressuring yields lower.

liquidity_pressure.reason: Easier liquidity can reduce stress-driven yield pressure but the channel is indirect.

treasury_yield_pressure.reason: Higher U.S. yields can support the dollar through rate-differential channels.

real_yield_pressure.reason: Higher real yields can support USD carry/relative return appeal.

inflation_pressure.reason: Hot U.S. inflation can support USD if it raises expected Fed restriction.

fed_hawkish_pressure.reason: A hawkish Fed path can support USD versus lower-yielding peers.

credit_stress.reason: Risk-off stress can support USD safe-haven demand, though pair-specific exceptions exist.

growth_strength.reason: Relative U.S. growth strength can support USD.

liquidity_pressure.reason: Easier liquidity can weaken defensive USD demand.

treasury_yield_pressure.reason: Higher U.S. yields generally support USD, pressuring non-USD/USD pairs.

real_yield_pressure.reason: Higher U.S. real yields generally support USD and pressure pairs quoted against USD.

inflation_pressure.reason: Hot U.S. inflation can pressure EURUSD/GBPUSD/AUDUSD if it raises Fed-rate expectations.

fed_hawkish_pressure.reason: A hawkish Fed path supports USD and pressures non-USD base currencies.

credit_stress.reason: Risk-off USD demand can pressure foreign currencies quoted against USD.

growth_strength.reason: U.S. growth strength can support USD, though the pair depends on relative foreign data.

liquidity_pressure.reason: Easier liquidity may reduce defensive USD demand and support risk-sensitive currencies.

real_yield_pressure.reason: Higher real yields are a major headwind for non-yielding precious metals.

treasury_yield_pressure.reason: Nominal yield pressure can hurt metals when real-yield or USD pressure dominates.

inflation_pressure.reason: Inflation pressure can support gold as an inflation hedge, but this can be offset by real yields.

fed_hawkish_pressure.reason: Hawkish policy can support real yields/USD and pressure gold.

credit_stress.reason: Risk-off or systemic stress can support safe-haven demand.

cot_metal_positioning.reason: COT participation can confirm or weaken metals pressure.

liquidity_pressure.reason: Easier liquidity can support non-yielding assets.

eia_energy_balance.reason: Energy inventories are not direct gold evidence.

usda_crop_stress.reason: Crop stress is not direct gold evidence.

eia_energy_balance.reason: Inventory draws, stronger product supplied, or tighter supply balances support energy pressure.

cot_energy_positioning.reason: Energy positioning can confirm participation or crowding.

growth_strength.reason: Growth strength can support demand-sensitive energy commodities.

credit_stress.reason: Credit/risk stress can pressure demand-sensitive commodities.

treasury_yield_pressure.reason: Higher rates can pressure demand/risk appetite but are not direct physical energy evidence.

inflation_pressure.reason: Energy can contribute to inflation, but CPI itself is not a direct oil inventory signal.

noaa_weather_hazard.reason: Weather can affect energy demand/supply depending on hazard type.

usda_crop_balance.reason: Tighter USDA supply/demand or crop condition pressure supports grain price pressure.

noaa_weather_hazard.reason: Drought, freeze, flood, heat, or storm hazards can affect crop supply risk.

cot_ag_positioning.reason: COT positioning can confirm or conflict with agricultural price pressure.

eia_energy_balance.reason: Energy costs can affect production/transport and biofuel channels, but this is indirect.

inflation_pressure.reason: Broad CPI is usually not direct grain evidence in this pass.

treasury_yield_pressure.reason: U.S. yields are not direct crop evidence.

growth_strength.reason: Demand context may matter but is not as direct as USDA/NOAA/COT.

default_rule.reason: No explicit asset/input mapping in v0.34; visible only unless manually mapped.
